# WhatTheDiff (wtd)

> Open-source, deterministic semantic diff CLI. Point it at N files ‚Äî configs,
> JSON, YAML, Markdown, logs ‚Äî and it reports what they agree on (consensus),
> how far each file deviates (drift), which files are outliers, and the exact
> evidence (file + line) behind every claim. Written in Zig; zero dependencies;
> static ~300 KB binaries for Linux, macOS, and Windows.

Key facts:
- Answers "what actually matters?" across a whole corpus, not "what changed?" between two files.
- Never compares raw text: files are decomposed into semantic primitives
  (e.g. `$.db.port=5432`), each hashed with BLAKE3, so key order, whitespace,
  quoting, and comments never register as differences.
- Consensus buckets: universal (in all N files), majority (2k > N),
  minority (1 < k, 2k ‚â§ N), unique (k = 1). The consensus core is every
  primitive held by a strict majority.
- Drift per file = 1 ‚àí Jaccard(its primitives, consensus core); outliers are
  flagged past mean + 1.5œÉ.
- Fully deterministic engine ‚Äî no AI, no randomness; byte-identical reports.
  Property-tested (seeded oracle suites) and benchmark-verified: ~23 ¬µs/file,
  flat scaling from 1,000 to 200,000 files; 4,000/4,000 planted outliers
  detected with zero false positives.
- `wtd --json` emits the full evidence graph (schema `wtd.report.v0`) for
  pipelines and AI explanation layers.

Install (checksum-verified):
- Linux/macOS/Git Bash: `curl -fsSL https://raw.githubusercontent.com/copyleftdev/whatthediff/main/install.sh | sh`
- Windows PowerShell: `irm https://raw.githubusercontent.com/copyleftdev/whatthediff/main/install.ps1 | iex`

Usage:
- `wtd <dir>` ‚Äî human report (consensus, drift ranking, outlier evidence)
- `wtd <dir> --drift` / `--consensus` ‚Äî single sections
- `wtd <dir> --json [--evidence]` ‚Äî machine-readable evidence graph
- `wtd ask "why is X different?" <dir>` ‚Äî an LLM explains the deterministic
  evidence with file:line citations; works with the Anthropic API
  (ANTHROPIC_API_KEY), OpenRouter (OPENROUTER_API_KEY), or any local
  OpenAI-compatible endpoint like Ollama (WTD_AI_URL + WTD_AI_MODEL, no key).
  The model only ever sees engine-generated evidence ‚Äî it cannot invent findings.
  `--dry-run` prints the exact prompt without calling a model.

## Links

- [Source repository](https://github.com/copyleftdev/whatthediff): Zig source, tests, benchmarks
- [README](https://github.com/copyleftdev/whatthediff/blob/main/README.md): full documentation
- [Design philosophy](https://github.com/copyleftdev/whatthediff/blob/main/intent.md): deterministic pipeline, evidence model, AI responsibilities
- [Releases](https://github.com/copyleftdev/whatthediff/releases): static binaries + SHA256SUMS for six platforms
- [Benchmark harness](https://github.com/copyleftdev/whatthediff/blob/main/scripts/bench.sh): reproduce the published numbers
